etymology.description: 子(子) (zǐ, "child") + 宗(宗) (zōng, "ancestral") used for phonetic value
etymology.interpretation: The character 㚇 combines 子(子) (zǐ, "child") with 宗(宗) (zōng, "ancestral") to form a phonetic compound primarily used to convey sound. The combination does not assign a unique semantic meaning but serves as a phonetic reference in compounds.
